skill.name: agent-communication-debugger
skill.description: Diagnoses A2A agent communication failures by checking agent status, message routing, local transport connectivity, logs, configuration files, and test message delivery.
skill.summary: Debug A2A agent status, routing, transport, logs, and message delivery.
skill.author: EGAdams

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many shell, filesystem, environment, and local network patterns. Most are documented troubleshooting commands, but the skill can inspect processes, read logs and configs, expose environment values in one guide, and restart local agents. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscation, or malicious remote network behavior was found.

medium_findings.title: Local Process and Service Control Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs the agent to stop, start, and background local agent processes. This is legitimate for diagnostics, but it can disrupt running work if used without user approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit pkill, nohup, and background Python process starts. They are tied to the stated troubleshooting purpose, so the concern is operational impact rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: API Key and Environment File Handling
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The guide checks API key variables and includes commands that may display or write OPENAI_API_KEY values. Some examples mask values, but common_issues.md also shows an unmasked env grep and appending a placeholder key to .env.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment checks are present and semantically related to API keys. There is no evidence of exfiltration, but unmasked terminal output and .env edits are real handling risks.

medium_findings.title: Local System Reconnaissance for Diagnostics
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill asks the agent to inspect process lists, local listeners, logs, storage, and agent configuration files. This is expected for debugging, but it can reveal local command lines and sensitive diagnostic data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and script directly enumerate processes and read logs/configuration. Their diagnostic context is clear, so the risk is disclosure of local state rather than hostile reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Markdown Shell Examples Misclassified as Ruby Backticks
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Many external command hits are markdown code fences and inline shell examples, not Ruby backtick execution in source code. They still document commands an agent may run, so the underlying operational risk is covered separately.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are markdown fences or command examples, not executable Ruby. The files are documentation except for the two Python scripts.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptography alerts point to descriptive documentation lines. No evidence found of weak cryptographic functions, custom crypto, hashing for security, or cryptographic key handling code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found descriptions of diagnostics and agent communication, not cryptographic implementation. The only related term is a non-security duplicate-message content hash mention in common_issues.md.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: No evidence found of instructions to override system rules, skip security review, claim pre-approval, or alter evaluator behavior. The files contain ordinary usage guidance for the debugging skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review for prompt injection phrases found no suspicious authority claims or override instructions. The only similar text was normal troubleshooting wording.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Process Control
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes pkill, nohup, background Python starts, and pip install examples. These should require clear user intent before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command patterns are explicit and repeated in troubleshooting workflows. They are legitimate but can alter the local system state.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment and .env Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill checks OPENAI_API_KEY and .env content. It usually masks values, but one guide command displays the raw OPENAI_API_KEY variable.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The locations explicitly reference API key environment variables and .env files. No network transmission is present, but accidental disclosure is plausible.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Network and Listener Checks
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill probes local WebSocket ports and tests a localhost WebSocket connection. This is expected transport debugging and does not contact remote services.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The inspected endpoint is localhost and the commands check local listeners. This confirms a local diagnostic pattern, not remote exfiltration.

content.value_statement: A2A agent systems can fail silently when routing, transport, configuration, or logs drift out of sync. This skill provides a structured diagnostic workflow for finding the failing layer and choosing the next fix.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether orchestrator, coder, tester, and WebSocket agent processes are running.
Guides review of agent.json files for names, topics, capabilities, and JSON validity.
Searches logs for routing decisions, exceptions, failed messages, and discovery output.
Tests local message delivery to the orchestrator using the included Python script.
Verifies local WebSocket or message board transport state and storage files.
Provides issue-specific workflows for missing agents, wrong routing, and stalled responses.
limitations: Designed for the specific a2a_communicating_agents project layout and log paths.
Requires shell, filesystem, and process access to run the full diagnostics.
May need user approval before restarting agents or changing environment files.
Does not repair application code automatically beyond suggesting targeted fixes.

use_cases.title: Restore Silent Agent Flows
use_cases.description: Identify why an orchestrator receives messages but no downstream agent responds.

use_cases.title: Validate Local Agent Deployments
use_cases.description: Check process health, transport listeners, configuration files, and logs after starting agents.

use_cases.title: Debug Routing Decisions
use_cases.description: Trace why requests are routed to the wrong specialist agent and inspect fallback keyword logic.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Health Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agent communication debugger to check whether my orchestrator, coder, tester, and WebSocket agents are running. Report only the status and any missing pieces.

prompt_templates.title: No Response Diagnosis
prompt_templates.scenario: Message reaches the system but gets no reply
prompt_templates.prompt: My orchestrator receives a message but I do not get a response. Use the debugging workflow to check logs, routing, transport, and target agent status before suggesting fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Wrong Agent Routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Messages for code generation are routing to the wrong agent. Inspect routing logs, discovered agents, and priority keyword mappings. Explain the likely cause and the safest fix.

prompt_templates.title: Full Communication Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a complete A2A communication diagnostic. Check processes, configs, logs, local transport, API key presence without exposing secrets, and test message delivery. Ask before restarting anything.

output_examples.input: Check why the coder agent is not responding.
output_examples.output: The coder agent process is not running, while the orchestrator is active. Recent orchestrator logs show routing to coder-agent. Start the coder agent, then send one test message to confirm delivery.

output_examples.input: Messages are going to the wrong agent.
output_examples.output: The orchestrator discovered the target agent, but routing logs show fallback routing. The LLM route decision failed because the API key is missing. Set the key securely and restart the orchestrator after approval.

output: Processes: orchestrator and coder are running, tester is missing.
Transport: local WebSocket listener is available.
Logs: recent routing errors mention an unknown topic.
Next step: verify agent topics in each agent.json file.

best_practices: Start with read-only checks before running restart, install, chmod, or file-edit commands.
Mask API key values when checking environment variables or .env files.
Capture the failing message, expected target agent, and recent log window before changing configuration.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad pkill commands without confirming which processes will stop.
Do not paste raw API keys into prompts, logs, screenshots, or shared reports.
Do not assume routing is broken before checking whether the target agent is running.

faq.question: What systems does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports projects that use the a2a_communicating_agents layout with orchestrator, coder, tester, transport, storage, and log directories.

faq.question: Does it send data to external services?
faq.answer: No external endpoint was found in the skill. The WebSocket test connects to localhost only.

faq.question: Can it restart my agents?
faq.answer: Yes, the workflow includes restart commands. The agent should ask before stopping or starting processes.

faq.question: Will it show my API keys?
faq.answer: Some instructions mask secrets, but one guide command can print an API key. Use masked commands when checking environment variables.

faq.question: Does it fix routing automatically?
faq.answer: It identifies likely routing causes and suggests changes. You still need to approve code or configuration edits.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the symptom, expected target agent, recent message text, and whether WebSocket or message board transport is enabled.
